title: Benchmark small models against larger baselines
canonical_summary: Small-model claims should be tested against larger baselines using the same prompts and retrieval conditions.
source_priority_rule: Evaluation results outrank expectations about model size.
answer_pattern: Explain paired benchmarking and why same-question comparisons matter.
exactness_guard: Do not claim 4B equals 7B without results.

title: Compare retrieval conditions separately
user_phrasing: no rag raw rag atlas rag
canonical_summary: Testing should separate no RAG, raw RAG, Atlas RAG, and Atlas Reader LoRA + Atlas RAG conditions.
source_priority_rule: The baseline matrix defines valid comparison conditions.
answer_pattern: List the comparison conditions and keep them separate.
exactness_guard: Do not mix conditions in a single result claim.

title: Small models need bounded tasks
canonical_summary: Small models should be tested on bounded lane/card tasks before broad synthesis tasks.
source_priority_rule: Small-model contract guidance defines the target use case.
answer_pattern: Explain that success is expected in bounded structured-RAG tasks first.
exactness_guard: Do not imply small models can solve all tasks.

title: Negative results are valid research data
canonical_summary: A failed or mixed benchmark still creates useful data about structured RAG, small-model routing, and adapter limits.
answer_pattern: Frame failure as data and identify what it teaches.
exactness_guard: Do not hide or relabel failed results as success.

title: Small model wins on format but loses on reasoning
user_phrasing: small model sounds good but wrong
canonical_summary: A small model may follow answer format while still failing deeper reasoning or evidence selection.
source_priority_rule: Rubric scores and raw outputs drive diagnosis.
exactness_guard: Do not score a polished answer as correct without evidence.

title: Large model wins without Atlas
canonical_summary: If a larger model without Atlas beats the small Atlas setup, record the result and inspect whether the task required deeper reasoning.
source_priority_rule: Measured results outrank the project hypothesis.
answer_pattern: Document where the larger model wins and why.
exactness_guard: Do not force the data to support the thesis.

title: Atlas improves retrieval but not final answer
canonical_summary: The system may retrieve better evidence while the model fails to apply it; this points to generation or adapter behavior rather than retrieval alone.
source_priority_rule: Retrieved context and output comparison drive branch.
answer_pattern: Separate retrieval success from answer-generation success.
exactness_guard: Do not declare Atlas failed if retrieval improved but generation did not.

title: Model-size limit vs retrieval failure
user_phrasing: is model too small or retrieval bad
canonical_summary: If relevant context was missing, inspect retrieval. If context was present but misused, inspect model size or adapter behavior.
source_priority_rule: Logs and retrieved context determine branch.
answer_pattern: Branch between retrieval and model-use failure.
exactness_guard: Do not blame model size before checking context.

title: Adapter benefit vs Atlas benefit
user_phrasing: did lora help or rag help
canonical_summary: Compare Atlas RAG without LoRA against Atlas RAG with LoRA to isolate adapter benefit.
source_priority_rule: Paired condition results determine whether LoRA added value.
answer_pattern: State which layer improved: retrieval structure or adapter behavior.
exactness_guard: Do not attribute all improvement to the LoRA.

title: Raw outputs and scores are source of truth
canonical_summary: For model benchmarking, saved raw outputs, scoring rubric, and test config outrank memory or screenshots.
answer_pattern: State the evidence required for a credible comparison.
exactness_guard: Do not rely on vibes or cherry-picked screenshots.

title: Do not confuse smaller-model usefulness with general equality
canonical_summary: A small model may become useful in bounded Atlas tasks without being generally equal to a larger model.
answer_pattern: Explain bounded usefulness vs global model parity.

title: How to answer benchmark questions
canonical_summary: Answers should name test conditions, report metrics, preserve failures, and avoid claims beyond the data.
answer_pattern: Use: condition -> result -> metric -> failure -> next decision.
exactness_guard: Do not summarize only the best cases.
